spec_match.rationale: The demo generates non-hyphenated CSS modifiers ('crossorigin' and 'referrerpolicy'), which contradicts the standard hyphenated specification ('cross-origin' and 'referrer-policy') demonstrated in parent index.html and modifier-builder.

interactive.rationale: The playground features interactive radio selectors for all three request modifiers that dynamically refresh the HTTP header mapping table and the output block.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Aligned perfectly with the parent description of showing exactly which headers are added or suppressed by each modifier.

edge_cases.rationale: Provides excellent edge-case details covering CORS mode parameters (Sec-Fetch-Mode), credentials (Cookie omission), and Referer paths.

references.rationale: The parent index contains the target ChromeStatus links, though a direct links list on this page is omitted.

design_system.rationale: Conforms nicely to the layout conventions, borders, and typographic styles of the showcase site.

openQuestions.title: Fix syntax hyphenation error in generated CSS modifiers
openQuestions.detail: The JavaScript script generating the CSS snippet in cors-header-playground/index.html outputs 'crossorigin()' and 'referrerpolicy()' instead of 'cross-origin()' and 'referrer-policy()'. This is a syntax error that differs from the spec and other concept pages. We should update lines 248-249 to use hyphenated function names so the generated code is valid.

openQuestions.title: Add references section with direct ChromeStatus and fetch spec links
openQuestions.detail: To improve reference alignment and build a better learning resource, we should append a references section linking directly to ChromeStatus and the Fetch / CSS Values specifications.

summary: A very useful security-oriented playground mapping CSS request modifiers to HTTP headers. However, it has a major spec conformance bug: the generated CSS output uses non-hyphenated 'crossorigin()' and 'referrerpolicy()' function names, which will fail to parse in supporting browsers. Fixing this hyphenation will make the demo highly compliant and complete.
